Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that filters out cases matching a specific direct succession sequence with a timing constraint 5 days between second and third event

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets break down the requirements 1 Event log table event_log with case_id activity timestamp 2 Identify cases with direct succession sequence Approve Purchase Requisition to Create Order to Confirm Order Direct succession means no other event from same case in between 3 Among those cases keep only those where time between Create Order and Confirm Order is 5 days 4 Exclude those cases from final result 5 Return all events from cases that do NOT meet the criteria

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Approach We can identify the bad cases using window functions or self joins then use NOT IN or anti join

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets think about direct succession For each case we need to check if there exists a triplet of events with these activities in this order with no other events in between

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: One approach For each event assign a row number per case ordered by timestamp Then check for pairs triples

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Specifically Event A activity Approve Purchase Requisition row num r Event B activity Create Order row_num r plus 1 Event C activity Confirm Order row_num r plus 2 This ensures direct succession if we order by timestamp and assume no ties

Name: Validation - IND
Text: But if there are ties same timestamp row_number might assign arbitrary order Better to use row_number over partition by case_id order by timestamp maybe some unique id if exists but only timestamp is mentioned Well assume timestamp ordering is sufficient and direct succession means consecutive in the ordered sequence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Then find cases where exists A at rn B at rn plus 1 with activity Create Order C at rn plus 2 with activity Confirm Order And A activity Approve Purchase Requisition

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Alternatively we can use LEAD LAG window functions Then filter where activity Approve Purchase Requisition and next act Create Order and next next act Confirm Order This is cleaner and avoids joins Lets use this

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So current Approve Purchase Requisition next lead 1 Create Order next2 lead 2 Confirm Order So the time between Create Order and Confirm Order is ts next2 minus ts next

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The lead by 1 and 2 over the ordered partition ensures that the next event is Create Order and the one after that is Confirm Order with no events in between in the ordered sequence This satisfies direct succession

Name: Validation - PE
Text: If a case contains the sequence with 5 days exclude it The distinct handles it

Name: Validation - IND
Text: One subtlety what if there are multiple sequences in a case The problem says contains the following sequence further refine where the time elapsed If a case contains the sequence with 5 days exclude it The distinct handles it

Name: Validation - IND
Text: What if there are multiple events with same timestamp The order by timestamp might not be deterministic but without a more granular ordering column this is the standard approach

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The sequence must be exactly these three in direct succession Our LEAD approach checks for any occurrence of Approve followed immediately by Create followed immediately by Confirm This is correct

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The problem says more than 5 days So greater than is correct
